_template_doc: BIE config template for full-marketing-audit v3.2.0. ARCHITECTURE PIVOT (2026-05-08): retired the bespoke MarketingAuditDashboard.tsx route in favor of Nick Snopov's single-custom-component-widget pattern (vault: Nick Snopov — HTML to Editable BI Dashboard Prompt 2026-05-07). The audit-paper visual is now a self-contained <style>+<script>+markup HTML document (audit-paper.html) embedded as the componentCode of ONE custom-component widget on a CrossChannelEditableDashboard. BIE skill-cli.ts:295 hardcodes dashboardUrl to CrossChannelEditableDashboard.tsx — this template now matches that hardcode (no fight). Pre-prod PR-preview deploys no longer block the audit because there is zero ai-dashboards code dependency beyond the generic editable-dashboard host that ships everywhere.
_slots_overview: Two placeholder strings to substitute before save: {{COMPONENT_CODE}} (the rendered audit-paper.html with __AUDIT_PAYLOAD_JSON__ replaced by JSON.stringify(payload)) and the title/tree/audit-date scalar slots. The widgets/layout arrays are NOT empty in v3.2.0 — they hold the single custom-component widget that renders the audit. The skill builds the auditPayload as before; it just no longer travels as a top-level config field — it's stringified into componentCode.
_lint_before_save: Zero {{...}} tokens remain anywhere in the saved JSON
componentCode contains the full audit-paper.html with __AUDIT_PAYLOAD_JSON__ already substituted to a valid JS object literal
componentCode does NOT contain a literal `</script>` outside of the closing tag at end of file (use JSON.stringify(payload).replace(/<\/script/gi, '<\\/script') when injecting)
componentCode size <= 100KB (Nick K3); audit-paper.html base is ~24KB so payload budget ~75KB
editState.widgets has exactly 1 entry, type='custom-component', renderMode='html', chromeless=true, gridWidth=12, gridHeight='full'
editState.layout.items has exactly 1 entry with the same id as the widget; static=true so the user can't drag-resize away the audit
appearance.layout.hideTitle=true, hideFilters=true, hideFooter=true so the audit-paper renders chromeless full-bleed
appearance.layout.background matches the audit-paper's --paper CSS var (#fafaf9) for seamless edges
dashboardUrl is the canonical CrossChannelEditableDashboard.tsx (BIE save would force this anyway, but be explicit)
dashboardTitle and dashboardTree have the audit_date_iso and client_or_agency_name substituted

_audit_payload_shape.client_or_agency_name: string — used in title + plain-English line
_audit_payload_shape.audit_window_start_utc_iso: string ISO — pinned 30d start, see SKILL idempotence rule 1
_audit_payload_shape.health_score: integer 0-100 — drives the gauge color zone
_audit_payload_shape.total_monthly_spend: integer USD (optional) — enables the % of monthly spend context line
_audit_payload_shape.n_paid_platforms: integer — count of live paid platforms
_audit_payload_shape.account_caption: string — terse Phase-1 summary, e.g. '5 active platforms · $296K/mo recoverable'
_audit_payload_shape.findings: array of finding objects (max 12 FAIL+WARN combined)
_audit_payload_shape.coverage_warning: string (optional) — set ONLY when audited_pct is in 0.40–0.79 partial branch (Phase 2.9). When set, the audit-paper renders a warning callout above the hero. When null/omitted, the callout is hidden (confident headline branch).

_finding_shape_example.headline: Zero conversion signals
_finding_shape_example.subline: Tennis Channel is spending $121,545/month on Meta AppSpecific subscription campaigns with zero measurable conversion events.
_finding_shape_example.observed: Account act_441508018218989: $121,545 in 30d across 4 campaign groups. mobile_app_install = 0, purchase = 0, lead = 0.
_finding_shape_example.fix: Verify Meta pixel fires on subscription confirmation screen. Create custom Subscribe event in Meta Events Manager. Pause AppSpecific campaigns until pixel verified.

_pass_blocked_shape_example.text: Account billing currency matches workspace default
